events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need diagnose fault: honeycomb from missed vibration in concrete placement in-service. Need decision fail because non-compliant/defective. Risk? Honeycomb can reduce durability, structural capacity depending extent. In heavy industrial asset, likely high if structural/pressure containment. But maybe medium? Need assess. Honeycomb from missed vibration indicates inadequate consolidation, resulting in voids, porous concrete, reduced strength and durability, potential corrosion ingress. In-service means operating asset, so defect exists. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, degraded. Maybe failed? Honeycomb is a defect but not necessarily failed unless severe. Use installed-defective and non-compliant, degraded. percent_complete 100 (in-service). value 0. confidence high 0.9. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty. findings: honeycomb/voids from inadequate vibration/consolidation; non-compliant with ACI 301/304. actions: evaluate extent via sounding/hammer/UT, remove and repair per ACI 301/304 and engineer, restore cover and durability. rationale. references ACI 301, ACI 304. Need ensure JSON valid.

  ],
  "actions": [
    "Assess extent and depth of honeycombing by sounding, hammer, or non-destructive testing",
    "Remove unsound concrete to sound substrate and repair using engineer-approved method compatible with ACI 301/304",
    "Verify reinforcement cover, bond, and durability after repair; re-inspect before returning to service"
  ],
  "rationale": "Honeycomb from missed vibration is a consolidation defect indicating the concrete was not properly placed and compacted per ACI requirements. In a heavy-industrial in-service asset it compromises structural integrity and durability, so the work fails inspection until evaluated and repaired.",
  "references": ["ACI 301", "ACI 304"]
}
